one_liner: Unified management service for EC2 and on-premises instances with Session Manager (secure shell access), Run Command (remote execution), Patch Manager, and Parameter Store.
when_to_use: Secure remote access to EC2 instances without opening SSH/RDP ports or managing bastion hosts (Session Manager)
Run commands or scripts across hundreds of instances simultaneously (Run Command)
Automated OS and application patching with compliance reporting (Patch Manager)
Store configuration values and secrets (Parameter Store, free tier available)
Inventory collection, software installation, and maintenance windows for fleet management
when_not_to_use: Application deployment and CI/CD (use CodeDeploy, CodePipeline)
Database credential rotation with native database integration (use Secrets Manager)
key_facts: Session Manager: browser-based or CLI shell access to EC2 instances. No inbound ports needed (uses SSM Agent outbound connection). Full audit logging to S3 and CloudWatch Logs. No SSH keys to manage.
Run Command: execute commands or scripts on multiple instances without SSH. Supports rate control (concurrency and error thresholds) for safe rolling execution.
Patch Manager: automate OS patching with patch baselines (define which patches to approve). Supports Linux and Windows. Reports compliance status.
Parameter Store: hierarchical key-value store for configuration data. Standard tier is free (up to 10,000 parameters). SecureString parameters encrypted with KMS.
Parameter Store vs. Secrets Manager: Parameter Store is free for standard tier and simpler. Secrets Manager costs $0.40/secret/month but provides native rotation and cross-account sharing.
Maintenance Windows: schedule automation tasks (patching, Run Command, Lambda) during defined time windows.
State Manager: define and enforce desired state configuration across instances (e.g., ensure antivirus is running, ensure specific packages are installed).
SSM Agent: pre-installed on Amazon Linux 2, Windows AMIs, and many marketplace AMIs. Required for all Systems Manager features.
Automation: orchestrate multi-step operational workflows (runbooks) for common tasks like AMI creation, snapshot management, and remediation.
OpsCenter: centralized location to view, investigate, and resolve operational issues across AWS resources.
Requires IAM instance profile with AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore policy for EC2 instances.
pricing_summary: Session Manager, Run Command, Patch Manager, State Manager: free. Parameter Store Standard: free (up to 10K params). Parameter Store Advanced: $0.05/parameter/month. Automation: free for most actions. OpsCenter: free for OpsItems.
common_misconceptions: Session Manager does NOT require SSH ports to be open. It uses the SSM Agent's outbound HTTPS connection. This is the recommended approach over bastion hosts.
Run Command is NOT the same as Patch Manager. Run Command executes any command or script. Patch Manager specifically manages OS patching with baselines and compliance.
Parameter Store is NOT just for secrets. It stores any configuration data (feature flags, DB connection strings, license keys). SecureString is for sensitive values.
Patch Manager can patch THIRD-PARTY software, not just OS patches. Custom patch baselines support third-party repositories.
SSM Agent must be running AND have internet/VPC endpoint access to Systems Manager. Instances without SSM Agent or connectivity will not appear in Systems Manager.
